design_decisions.summary: Diff opts struct vs reusing VendorOpts — Diff gets its own DiffOpts with SourceDir + RepoRoot, narrower interface, self-documenting that this isn't a vendor operation
design_decisions.justification: The contract is different (returns []Divergence, never writes), and adding CheckOnly to a diff opts would be misleading.

design_decisions.summary: Divergence type — File (embed-relative path) + Reason (short descriptor)
design_decisions.justification: The CLI prints <file>: <reason> per line; the spec asks for file path plus a short reason.

design_decisions.summary: Reusing the Vendor transform/mapping code paths exactly — Diff calls the same batonFileMappings, ValidateSource, and Transform that Vendor uses
design_decisions.justification: Spec Risk #1 — any code-path fork makes a clean tree show false divergence.

design_decisions.summary: Exit code convention — 0=in sync, 1=divergent, 64=usage error, consistent with sworn baton vendor
design_decisions.justification: The sworn CLI already uses 64 for usage errors; 1 for divergence is unambiguous.

design_decisions.summary: ADR-0006 finalisation — confirm no open question remains and note in proof.md that the enforcement the ADR describes now exists; no edit to ADR file itself unless an open question is found (none is)
design_decisions.justification: ADR is already Status: accepted; finalisation means confirming enforcement exists.

open_deferrals.item: CI wiring for sworn baton diff
open_deferrals.why: CI configuration is a separate harness change; this slice delivers the diff command and governance doc

open_deferrals.item: Live-remote diff (network fetch of upstream tag)
open_deferrals.why: Network fetch boundary requires S62-baton-upstream-source infrastructure
